card.intuition: The CSS Object Model is a map of all CSS selectors and relevant properties for every node in the DOM, used by the browser to calculate the final styles.
card.analogy: A set of 'styling instructions' for the building blueprint: if the DOM says 'This is a wall', the CSSOM says 'All walls should be blue and 10 feet tall'.
when_to_use: Optimizing page load time (CSSOM construction blocks rendering)
Understanding how 'cascading' and 'specificity' work under the hood
Debugging why a specific style isn't being applied
gotchas: CSSOM construction is 'Render-Blocking' — the browser cannot start painting until it has the full CSSOM
Inline styles and external CSS files are all merged into this single object model

exercise.prompt: Does the browser build the DOM and CSSOM in parallel or sequentially?
exercise.starter_code:
def building_process():
    # They are built _____________, but both are needed for the Render Tree
    order = "parallel"
    return order
hints: The browser doesn't wait for HTML to finish before starting on CSS it found
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "parallel"
assert check("parallel")
print("PASS")
